message:
refactor(three-scene): improve seededRandom with Mulberry32 algorithm

Replace the sine-based pseudo-random number generator with Mulberry32,
a well-tested PRNG with superior statistical properties.

Previous implementation (sine-based):
- Used Math.sin(x) * 10000 to generate pseudo-random values
- Simple but has known statistical weaknesses
- Some seed values could produce short cycles or correlated sequences

New implementation (Mulberry32):
- Uses 32-bit integer arithmetic with XOR shifts and multiplication
- Provides excellent statistical distribution (passes most randomness tests)
- Long period with no observable short cycles
- Consistent performance across all seed values
- Uses bitwise operations for fast execution

Algorithm details:
  state = (state + 0x6d2b79f5) | 0;
  let t = Math.imul(state ^ (state >>> 15), 1 | state);
  t = (t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), 61 | t)) ^ t;
  return ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;

Changes:
- Export seededRandom function for external use/testing
- Update unit tests to import from module instead of duplicating
- Update expected values in determinism tests to match new algorithm

The seededRandom function is used for:
- Generating deterministic star field positions in 3D scenes
- Creating reproducible layouts for various scene variants
- Ensuring consistent visual appearance across page loads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
